mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving immediate equity risk premium compression and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.5, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and new housing starts signals broad-based economic acceleration that boosts corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days only when both series expand concurrently.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative SPX returns in more than 60% of observed instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, sustained industrial production growth indicates capacity utilization peaking, which historically precedes margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of late-cycle instances where INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5 percent quarter-over-quarter, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during post-2008 data periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising revolving credit balances reflect increasing consumer leverage and spending momentum, which directly supports corporate sales forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative SPX returns in more than 55% of rolling 12-month windows, or the correlation between RSAFS month-over-month changes and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate increases by more than 0.25 percentage points within a single month, the abrupt tightening of financial conditions compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the rate hike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently rising unemployment and accelerating consumer price inflation signal stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive policy response, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive SPX returns in more than 55% of instances where both series move adversely, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC reduction < 2) over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market booms, rapid growth in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for construction-related corporate earnings and wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative in more than 50% of housing boom quarters where HOUST growth exceeds 10 percent year-over-year, or the signal's predictive power disappears after controlling for interest rate changes.
